item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Series 1 vibes, I like it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Boo!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Once it activates, you can't let it go, then the object become immovable, and it will not only eat you with a hole that gets bigger than the object itself, but you will anomalously be unable to die so the article can be more horrific. And no trace of the victim will be found. Yeah, that means the precontainment mayhem was even worse, but doesn't make much sense for the item. Finally it was recovered from an elementary school so we can recoil about all the dead children.

Boo!

(I'm gonna go read what else the author wrote, since people liked it as a good first effort.

Edit: Didn't have any other articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a wood chipper masquerading as a pencil sharpener. As others have said it's a 'thing wot kills people' with no real draw other than the "Oh God think of the children!" final line. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Old style is old, and doesn't live up to current standards.

Which is a shame, because that last line is a perfect kicker.

-1, but I hope it gets a rewrite. Not even a full re-write, just a "re-polish" to freshen up the general tone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
OTT pencil-sharpner what eats you, and you expunged something for no reason at all. But that last paragraph… that's some good horror material.
No vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: standard

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a very simple SCP, but it's short enough it gets away with it. I think this one's real strength is that it taps into the kind of irrational childhood fear that many folks may have forgotten they ever had.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm in the same camp as Lurk. It's a lackluster skip, but the last line makes up for all of it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Only reason you're getting my upvote is for that last sentence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dull, just a thing wot kills you in a manner thematically linked to the object's forum.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alright, I updated the article with a picture, and updated/reworked some of the grammar as well. I slightly modified the description of the SCP to justify the picture (originally it said that blood that was touching the sharpener vanished and the picture that was provided only makes sense in a pre-recovery environment, so I had it also disappear blood near it)

Finally, I altered the final line slightly to censor the name of the elementary school, and to remove the redaction. The final line definitely feels ham-fisted in hindsight, but damn I'm still too proud of it to let it go.

Edit: Also, before I forget, here is the image I added. The picture is provided via Wikimedia by Vacuumfan7072 and is licensed under Creative Commons. Thanks WrongJohnSilver for linking it. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooh, yeah, that's perfect! I'll go ahead and make the modifications. Thank you. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You mean like this one? It's got the CC license.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the only SCP I've ever written, and I'm pretty proud of it, but the fact that it doesn't have an image anymore bugs me. If anybody wants to come in and place a new image that's actually allowed (and update the visual description of the SCP), feel free. I would request that only the visual description be updated and not the containment procedures though, even though I know they're not entirely up to standard. (They wouldn't be the only things in the first two series not up to standard, I don't think it requires modification)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, the story just petered out without a real payoff, didn't it? OTT thing what kills you. No save.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah. I mean, the idea is taken nowhere. Are we supposed to upvote because, "Oh noes teh children!" Are we supposed to upvote because, "It could happen to anyone, even YOU!"? This isn't effective as a piece of horror. I kept waiting for the twist to come, and it never did.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's nothing interesting about this. It's just an object. -1.

item.title: Image removal
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1307's image was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. It may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact Fantem or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.

Source:
http://arttoys.wordpress.com/2011/07/18/pencil-pointers-the-foray-electric-sharpener/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one a lot. It's simple but effective - not many SCPs actually manage to horrify me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1307 was recovered at Rosewood Elementary in ███████████, Colorado after [DATA EXPUNGED]

You sick bastard, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One question, did anyone try cutting the pencil at any point in time? I mean, it said the only way to remove the hand from the pencil was amputation, but couldn't they damage the pencil enough, through snapping or cutting? It wasn't detailed and it isn't required, but it could be interesting if put it experiment logs or the like. Just an opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty simple and effective. Could stand a bit of spit-polish, but that's about it. This does what it does, and what it does is pretty terrifying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I knew it. I always knew that pencil sharpener in middle school was planning something.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fuck it, I like this one.

I don't wanna be eaten by a pencil sharpener.

item.title: Re: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: I always liked this one even though it's rather simple. Just clicked with some childhood fears. The redaction at the end could be changed, if other people think the rating justifies changing it, but I think it works pretty well enough as is.

item.title: Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
This article has been placed up for review. Please check it our, it could use more attention

While this didn't impress me at first, the bit where it got to "4 hours to consume a human body" changed my downvote to a neutral vote. With a bit more detail and polish, this could easily be a classic body horror/squick scp.

That redaction at the end is hella dumb though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Impressive. Short, sweet, and to the point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: JUST a pencil sharpener that eats people?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh dear god this is horrifying. You tickled my childhood fear. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is no need for that redaction; knowing it was found in an elementary school is enough. There had to be some reason the Foundation was alerted, of course…

You've managed to hit on a childhood fear well enough that you get the +1 anyway, damn you. Over The Top Stuff What Kills You still works if you're playing into the right fears.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was the fact that it was found after [REDACTED] that made me upvote this. Not bad for a first SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Rather simple idea, but I find that this SCP does what many other do to much: make unassuming things murderers. It isn't especially effective at striking any emotions other than the "that's fucked up" feeling.

The execution was good. Nothing was over-the-top, and you didn't feel obligated to explain to much and you didn't leave any plot-holes. This is a good foundation for more complicated SCPs down the road. Upvote because this is a good stepping stone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm… my only problem with this is that last expungement. It seems like something that was put in to censor the squick, rather than something that was covered up because it is a new, sensitive scientific discovery. That being said, I don't know if it would benefit the article to actually include that expunged info in there, or to even have a recovery in the article at all. Judging by previous comments, I would say leave the origin in there, include just a little bit more info to get readers' minds going in a certain direction and not add much more than that. Keep it vague.

Other than that, great job. Good first article. (Upvoted in order to keep it around so that more people can see it, and potentially offer you more advice to take this from "good" to "awesome".)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For a first SCP this is….damned good, actually.

You dodged most of the first-timer bullets, and the ones that did tag you aren't the game-breakers. It's not a wall of text, it's not a superhero, it's not an scp generator, and it doens't feel like you've tacked anything on after the fact to make it "better".

The only thing I can find to critique is small, subtle, and maybe just my opinion. For the last line, you can put a period after Colorado. Knowing it was in a school is more than enough. We don't need to know that some censored incident happened, any reader will assume that it did. As a general rule of thumb, every instance of expungement, redaction, or black boxes you can cut should be cut.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good catch. Fixed. Just a heads up to anybody else: if you see any more spelling errors, feel free to correct them. If you see any bad grammar or awkwardly worded sentences, though, let me know. I'd prefer to reword such things myself! Thanks. :'D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Success! A friend suggested that I add where it came from, but had no idea, so he suggested a school of some kind. It creeped me out so bad that I knew I needed to add it right away.

I personally like to imagine that it's a school for orphans. Somehow the over the top-ness of that idea makes it a bit funnier so I can sleep at night. YMMV.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have a pencil sharpener like that… It is in my classroom. My students use it every day. Upvoted. One little thing, it's spelled Colorado, not Colarado.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Overall concept is…meh-ish, but you get an upvote for horrifying me with the origin. Knowing what it does and having it found in an elementary school after some kind of incident? Enough said. I like the fact that you went, "Yeah, I'm pretty sure I don't need to spell this out for them" and just expunged it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even though it's an OTT pencil sharpener what eats you… I like it. It freaks me the hell out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I cringed involuntarily.

Enjoy your vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's… Well, a fucking evil pencil sharpener. Not really over-the-top, since it isn't just making the pencil so sharp it can pass through the earth or anything like that. It isn't just a thing that eats you, since… well, it's more torture than eating. You know, being ground to a pulp, in a literal fashion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, I was actually really nervous that I would be buried under a ton of downvotes like I was warned my first one would be, but I guess I did a pretty good job with the writing, so thanks for the support.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How so? I'm not shooting down the idea, but what's your reasoning?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Main part of the article that grated on me. It really shreds up what credibility it otherwise had. I advise cutting this line off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did it blow my mind? No. Is it an OTT but otherwise simple thing? Yes.
Do I still like it?
…..
Yes. Especially as a first entry, not too shabby.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You'll wanna put that in a collapsible, bro. No unspoilered images in the forums.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm about to add a sentence to the end that may or may not change your mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it somewhat. It's a simple and effective article, with enough fridge horror to save it from taking the wrong turn in downvote junction.

However, it's not enough to elicit any upvote from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
nope.avi

(on my iPhone at the moment so this'll have to do))

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ^dude knows what's up

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For Science!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The effect will not be activated by pens, pencil-shaped steel rods, or [REDACTED].

Penises?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: nopenopenope

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not terrible, but it's just a pencil sharpener that eats people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Success! I'm totally playing on a fear of sticking your fingers into a sharpener. And, SCP, of course it's gonna be a bit ridiculous. :v

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Blah. On the one hand, this is classic Thing What Eats You, and it's really not that interesting in addition to being a bit ridiculous. On the other hand, this plays into a gut-level fear of mine well enough that I legitimately cringed, so I can't downvote in good conscience. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Success! I was hoping that would happen to somebody. I almost used the model on my desk currently, but it freaked me out a bit too much.

Edit: Also, you may be my brother. We should research this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yikes!

I have a pencil sharpener which looks just like your picture for this SCP on my desk. Right next to the computer. As I type this. Needless to say I found this somewhat creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty 'meh'.

No vote.
